text.content: 1.1  Graph Reading With Scale 2  [s1_1_graph_reading_with_scale_2]

rich_text.plain_text: 1.1  Graph Reading With Scale 2  [s1_1_graph_reading_with_scale_2]

text.content: Horizontal bar graph appears. After-School Activities data. Soccer=12, Basketball=7, Tennis=6, Swimming=11. Scale of 2.

rich_text.plain_text: Horizontal bar graph appears. After-School Activities data. Soccer=12, Basketball=7, Tennis=6, Swimming=11. Scale of 2.

text.content: "Let's warm up your graph skills. Here's a bar graph. Check the scale."

rich_text.plain_text: "Let's warm up your graph skills. Here's a bar graph. Check the scale."

text.content:
tool: multiple_choice  options: [6, 10, 12, 24]
"How many students chose soccer?"

rich_text.plain_text:
tool: multiple_choice  options: [6, 10, 12, 24]
"How many students chose soccer?"

text.content: ✅ Student answered 12, correct

rich_text.plain_text: ✅ Student answered 12, correct

text.content: Soccer bar highlights to confirm selection.

rich_text.plain_text: Soccer bar highlights to confirm selection.

text.content: "Right. 12 students. The scale goes by 2s. Each mark on the axis shows 2 students."

rich_text.plain_text: "Right. 12 students. The scale goes by 2s. Each mark on the axis shows 2 students."

text.content: ❌ Student selected 6, which is where the Soccer bar ends but ignores the scale of 2

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student selected 6, which is where the Soccer bar ends but ignores the scale of 2

text.content: Soccer bar highlights. Scale axis emphasized.

rich_text.plain_text: Soccer bar highlights. Scale axis emphasized.

text.content: "You counted where the bar ends. But look at the scale: each mark stands for 2 students, not 1. Count by 2s."

rich_text.plain_text: "You counted where the bar ends. But look at the scale: each mark stands for 2 students, not 1. Count by 2s."

text.content: ❌ Student selected 10, close to correct but miscounted the intervals

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student selected 10, close to correct but miscounted the intervals

text.content: Soccer bar highlights. Scale markings emphasized.

rich_text.plain_text: Soccer bar highlights. Scale markings emphasized.

text.content: "Almost. The scale goes by 2s. Count each mark carefully: 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12. Where does the Soccer bar end?"

rich_text.plain_text: "Almost. The scale goes by 2s. Count each mark carefully: 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12. Where does the Soccer bar end?"

text.content: ❌ Student selected 24, which doubles the correct answer by counting each interval as 2 rather than each mark representing 2

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student selected 24, which doubles the correct answer by counting each interval as 2 rather than each mark representing 2

text.content: Soccer bar highlights. Scale axis emphasized.

rich_text.plain_text: Soccer bar highlights. Scale axis emphasized.

text.content: "That number is too high. The scale tells us what each mark means. Each mark on the axis shows 2 students. Count the marks where the Soccer bar ends."

rich_text.plain_text: "That number is too high. The scale tells us what each mark means. Each mark on the axis shows 2 students. Count the marks where the Soccer bar ends."

text.content: ❌ Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

text.content: Soccer bar highlights. System demonstrates counting by 2s along the scale.

rich_text.plain_text: Soccer bar highlights. System demonstrates counting by 2s along the scale.

text.content: "Let me show you. The scale goes by 2s: 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12. The Soccer bar reaches the 12 mark. That means 12 students chose soccer. The answer is 12 because the scale tells us each mark represents 2 students, not 1."

rich_text.plain_text: "Let me show you. The scale goes by 2s: 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12. The Soccer bar reaches the 12 mark. That means 12 students chose soccer. The answer is 12 because the scale tells us each mark represents 2 students, not 1."

text.content: 2.1  Comparison Question Single Step  [s2_1_comparison_question_single_step]

rich_text.plain_text: 2.1  Comparison Question Single Step  [s2_1_comparison_question_single_step]

text.content: Vertical bar graph appears. Favorite Sports data. Soccer 12, Basketball 7, Tennis 6, Swimming 11.

rich_text.plain_text: Vertical bar graph appears. Favorite Sports data. Soccer 12, Basketball 7, Tennis 6, Swimming 11.

text.content: "Now a question about this data. How many more students chose soccer than tennis?"

rich_text.plain_text: "Now a question about this data. How many more students chose soccer than tennis?"

text.content:
tool: multiple_choice  options: [18, 12, 6, 3]
"How many more students chose soccer than tennis?"

rich_text.plain_text:
tool: multiple_choice  options: [18, 12, 6, 3]
"How many more students chose soccer than tennis?"

text.content: ✅ Student selected 6, correct (12 - 6 = 6)

rich_text.plain_text: ✅ Student selected 6, correct (12 - 6 = 6)

text.content: Soccer and Tennis bars highlight to confirm the comparison.

rich_text.plain_text: Soccer and Tennis bars highlight to confirm the comparison.

text.content: "Right. 6 more. How many more means compare. We subtract. Soccer is 12, tennis is 6, difference is 6."

rich_text.plain_text: "Right. 6 more. How many more means compare. We subtract. Soccer is 12, tennis is 6, difference is 6."

text.content: ❌ Student selected 18: added soccer and tennis instead of finding the difference

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student selected 18: added soccer and tennis instead of finding the difference

text.content: Soccer and Tennis bars highlight.

rich_text.plain_text: Soccer and Tennis bars highlight.

text.content: "You added soccer and tennis. The question asks how many more, which means we need to find the difference. Subtract the smaller number from the larger one."

rich_text.plain_text: "You added soccer and tennis. The question asks how many more, which means we need to find the difference. Subtract the smaller number from the larger one."

text.content: ❌ Student selected 12: only read soccer's value instead of comparing

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student selected 12: only read soccer's value instead of comparing

text.content: Soccer and Tennis bars highlight.

rich_text.plain_text: Soccer and Tennis bars highlight.

text.content: "That's how many chose soccer. The question asks how many more chose soccer than tennis. Compare the two bars. What is the difference between their heights?"

rich_text.plain_text: "That's how many chose soccer. The question asks how many more chose soccer than tennis. Compare the two bars. What is the difference between their heights?"

text.content: ❌ Student selected 3: possibly subtracted 6 from 12 incorrectly or confused the values

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student selected 3: possibly subtracted 6 from 12 incorrectly or confused the values

text.content: Soccer and Tennis bars highlight.

rich_text.plain_text: Soccer and Tennis bars highlight.

text.content: "Not quite. Soccer has 12 students, tennis has 6. To find how many more, subtract. What is 12 minus 6?"

rich_text.plain_text: "Not quite. Soccer has 12 students, tennis has 6. To find how many more, subtract. What is 12 minus 6?"

text.content: ❌ Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

text.content: Soccer and Tennis bars highlight to demonstrate the comparison.

rich_text.plain_text: Soccer and Tennis bars highlight to demonstrate the comparison.

text.content: "Let me show you. How many more means we compare by finding the difference. Soccer is 12, tennis is 6. Subtract: 12 minus 6 equals 6. The answer is 6. That's what the difference between the two bars shows."

rich_text.plain_text: "Let me show you. How many more means we compare by finding the difference. Soccer is 12, tennis is 6. Subtract: 12 minus 6 equals 6. The answer is 6. That's what the difference between the two bars shows."

text.content: 3.1  Quick Scale Check Lesson Context  [s3_1_quick_scale_check_lesson_context]

rich_text.plain_text: 3.1  Quick Scale Check Lesson Context  [s3_1_quick_scale_check_lesson_context]

text.content: Vertical bar graph appears. Favorite Lunch data. Scale of 5. Pizza=45, Tacos=25, Salad=10, Burgers=15.

rich_text.plain_text: Vertical bar graph appears. Favorite Lunch data. Scale of 5. Pizza=45, Tacos=25, Salad=10, Burgers=15.

text.content: "Different graph, different scale. Quick check."

rich_text.plain_text: "Different graph, different scale. Quick check."

text.content:
tool: multiple_choice  options: [9, 25, 45, 50]
"How many students chose pizza?"

rich_text.plain_text:
tool: multiple_choice  options: [9, 25, 45, 50]
"How many students chose pizza?"

text.content: ✅ Student answered 45, correct, 9 marks × 5

rich_text.plain_text: ✅ Student answered 45, correct, 9 marks × 5

text.content: Pizza bar highlights to confirm selection.

rich_text.plain_text: Pizza bar highlights to confirm selection.

text.content: "Right. 45 students. Scale of 5, you've got it."

rich_text.plain_text: "Right. 45 students. Scale of 5, you've got it."

text.content: ❌ Student selected 9: counted the marks instead of multiplying by scale

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student selected 9: counted the marks instead of multiplying by scale

text.content: Pizza bar highlights.

rich_text.plain_text: Pizza bar highlights.

text.content: "You counted the marks on the bar. But look at the scale: each mark stands for 5 students. How many students is that?"

rich_text.plain_text: "You counted the marks on the bar. But look at the scale: each mark stands for 5 students. How many students is that?"

text.content: ❌ Student selected 25: read wrong category or miscounted

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student selected 25: read wrong category or miscounted

text.content: Pizza and Tacos bars highlight.

rich_text.plain_text: Pizza and Tacos bars highlight.

text.content: "That's the Tacos bar. We need Pizza. Count the marks on the Pizza bar and multiply by 5."

rich_text.plain_text: "That's the Tacos bar. We need Pizza. Count the marks on the Pizza bar and multiply by 5."

text.content: ❌ Student selected 50: read axis maximum instead of bar height

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student selected 50: read axis maximum instead of bar height

text.content: Pizza bar highlights.

rich_text.plain_text: Pizza bar highlights.

text.content: "That's the top of the axis. Count where the Pizza bar stops. How many marks does it reach?"

rich_text.plain_text: "That's the top of the axis. Count where the Pizza bar stops. How many marks does it reach?"

text.content: ❌ Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

rich_text.plain_text: ❌ Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

text.content: System counts marks on Pizza bar: 9 marks highlighted sequentially.

rich_text.plain_text: System counts marks on Pizza bar: 9 marks highlighted sequentially.

text.content: "Let me show you. I count the marks on the Pizza bar: 9 marks. The scale says each mark equals 5 students. 9 times 5 equals 45. The answer is 45 students. When the scale isn't 1, you multiply the marks by the scale number."

rich_text.plain_text: "Let me show you. I count the marks on the Pizza bar: 9 marks. The scale says each mark equals 5 students. 9 times 5 equals 45. The answer is 45 students. When the scale isn't 1, you multiply the marks by the scale number."
